warnings: symmetry_number defaulted to 1; if the molecule has rotational symmetry (e.g. H2O σ=2, NH3 σ=3, CH4 σ=12) the entropy and Gibbs energy are over-estimated by R·ln(σ). Re-run with --symmetry-number set to the correct value for your point group.

prose:
Property: electronic total energy (E_electronic) of ferrocene
Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding)
Geometry provenance: user-supplied ferrocene.xyz; no pre-optimization per --no-preopt, so the Hessian and energy were evaluated at that fixed geometry (single-point frequency calculation, i.e. vib-analysis//input geometry)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet)
Software: xtb via chemkit vibrational-analysis skill (backend xtb 6.x)
Convergence & caveats: Hessian diagonalization yielded 0 imaginary modes → stationary minimum; SCF cycles converged (status ok). Density-fitting inherently used by GFN2-xTB. Warning echoed verbatim below.
Live log: the run streamed to a live .out file in the working directory while executing; path is reproduced in the CLI invocation recorded in the JSON.

Headline result (gas-phase E_electronic): –804.80517 eV

The vibrational-analysis task also returns ZPE-corrected enthalpy (H) and Gibbs free energy (G), but per the user request only the electronic energy is reported as the headline value.
